title: Priority Rotation Reset on Idle
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [FAIRNESS] Master 0 granted 100% after idle period
testbench: task check_fairness(); // Drive M3, then IDLE, then M3 again endtask
expected_root_cause: The arbiter resets the 'last_gnt' pointer to 0 whenever the bus is idle, causing it to lose its fairness state and always start with Master 0.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if (!rst_n) last_gnt <= 0; else if (|gnt) last_gnt <= current_id;
description: Arbiter forgets fairness state during idle cycles, leading to Master 0 bias.

title: Priority Pointer Wrap-Around Error
expected_root_cause: Binary pointer increments past the actual number of masters (3), reaching 2'b11 (3), which doesn't map to any master in a 3-master system.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (gnt_done) last_gnt <= (last_gnt == NUM_MASTERS-1) ? 0 : last_gnt + 1;
description: Round-robin pointer enters out-of-bounds state in non-power-of-two master configurations.

title: Combinational Grant Glitch
rtl: assign gnt = req && !last_gnt; assign gnt[5] = req[5] && last_gnt;
expected_root_cause: Grant signals are purely combinational and react to intermediate transitions in the priority mask logic before the clock edge.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) begin gnt <= next_gnt_comb; end
description: Combinational path in arbiter causes illegal multi-master grant pulses during rotation.

title: Sticky Grant Violation
expected_root_cause: The grant logic switches immediately on a new high-priority request rather than waiting for the AXI-Lite BVALID response.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (active && !bvalid) gnt <= gnt; else gnt <= calculated_next_gnt;
description: Arbiter drops grant mid-transaction when a higher priority request appears.

title: Non-One-Hot Grant on Reset Exit
rtl: always_ff @(posedge clk) gnt <= next_gnt; // No reset
testbench: initial begin rst_n = 0; req = 2'b11; #20 rst_n = 1; end
expected_root_cause: Grant registers are uninitialized (X) during reset, and the first valid clock edge grants both requests due to indeterminate mask state.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if (!rst_n) gnt <= 0; else gnt <= next_gnt;
description: Initialization failure leads to bus contention immediately after reset deassertion.

title: Starvation via Mask Inversion
rtl: assign masked_req = req & ~mask; // mask is always 4'b1111
expected_root_cause: The priority mask is inverted incorrectly or hardcoded, effectively blocking the highest-indexed master regardless of history.
fix_replacement: always_comb begin mask = (1 << last_gnt) - 1; masked_req = req & ~mask; end
description: Static masking prevents specific masters from ever winning arbitration.

title: Blocking Assignment in Priority Update
bug_signature: VCS-ERROR: Race condition in arbiter state
expected_root_cause: Use of '=' in a sequential block causes simulation to update the priority state in the same delta cycle, resulting in double-rotation.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) last_gnt <= current_gnt;
description: Simulation/synthesis mismatch due to incorrect assignment type in sequential logic.

title: Grant-without-Request Fault
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [PROT] Spurious GNT detected for Master 2
expected_root_cause: The arbiter defaults to granting Master 2 when the bus is idle, even if Master 2 hasn't requested access, violating AXI master ready rules.
fix_replacement: always_comb begin gnt = 0; if (req[last_gnt]) gnt[last_gnt] = 1; ... end
description: Arbiter drives valid grant signals to masters that are not requesting the bus.

title: Unmapped State Deadlock
testbench: initial begin force state = 5; #10 release state; end
expected_root_cause: State machine lacks a default case, trapping the arbiter in an undefined state if a glitch occurs.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) case(state) ... default: state <= IDLE; endcase
description: FSM deadlock caused by missing default recovery logic.

title: Asynchronous Clear Hazard
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_XZ] GNT is X during clear deassertion
rtl: always_ff @(posedge clk or posedge clr) if(clr) gnt <= 0;
expected_root_cause: The clear signal is handled asynchronously without synchronization, creating a setup violation on the grant registers.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) begin clr_q1 <= clr; clr_q2 <= clr_q1; end
description: Metastability in grant signals due to asynchronous control path.

title: Double-Grant on Priority Flip
expected_root_cause: Combinational logic for grant generation does not enforce mutual exclusion between the primary and secondary priority paths.
fix_replacement: always_comb begin if (req) gnt = 2'b01; else if (req[5]) gnt = 2'b10; end
description: Mutual exclusion failure in arbiter resulting in multiple simultaneous grants.

title: Grant Revocation on Wait-State
testbench: task check(); drive_req(M0); force wait_sig = 1; endtask
expected_root_cause: The arbiter removes the grant signal if the slave asserts a wait state, violating the rule that a grant must remain stable until accepted.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (gnt && wait_sig) gnt <= gnt; else gnt <= next_gnt;
description: Premature grant removal during slave backpressure.

title: Missing Implicit Width in Pointer
rtl: logic [1:0] last_gnt; // Needs 3 bits for 8 masters
expected_root_cause: The 'last_gnt' register width is too narrow, causing the pointer to wrap after Master 3 and skip Masters 4-7.
fix_replacement: localparam PTR_W = $clog2(NUM_MASTERS); logic [PTR_W-1:0] last_gnt;
description: Fairness failure where half the masters are never served due to bit-width truncation.

title: Round-Robin Skip-Ahead Bug
expected_root_cause: The rotation logic increments by more than one, causing the arbiter to skip every other master in the sequence.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (any_gnt) last_gnt <= (last_gnt + 1) % 4;
description: Logic error in pointer increment results in deterministic unfairness.

title: One-Hot Case with Multi-Bit Input
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [SVA_1HOT] Case statement collision on REQ=4'b1100
expected_root_cause: Using 'unique case(1'b1)' with a multi-bit request vector triggers a simulator error if multiple requests are high, as 'unique' forbids multiple matches.
fix_replacement: priority case(1'b1) req: ... req[5]: ... endcase
description: Verification check failure where valid concurrent requests cause a runtime crash.

title: Grant Pulse on Reset Deassertion
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [RST_PROT] Grant high for 1ns at reset exit
expected_root_cause: The reset-mux logic drives a high value to grants during the reset phase or the cycle immediately after.
fix_replacement: always_comb if (!rst_n) gnt = 0; else gnt = next_gnt;
description: Illegal bus activity during system initialization.

title: Self-Locking Arbiter State
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [TIMEOUT] Arbiter never grants after M0 disconnects
expected_root_cause: State machine logic requires 'last_gnt' to be a value other than its current state to transition, creating a deadlock if the current master stops requesting.
fix_replacement: always_comb begin next_state = IDLE; if (req[5]) next_state = GNT1; else if (req) next_state = GNT0; end
description: Arbiter deadlock where a master's departure freezes the rotation logic.

title: Implicit Priority Inversion
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [FAIRNESS] Master 1 always wins over Master 0
rtl: assign gnt[5] = req[5]; assign gnt = req && !req[5];
expected_root_cause: The logic is hardcoded for Master 1 priority, ignoring the round-robin state entirely.
fix_replacement: always_comb if (last_gnt == 0) begin if (req[5]) gnt=2; else gnt=1; end
description: Arbiter behaves as fixed-priority despite being labeled as round-robin.

title: Round-Robin Clock-Domain Crossing Error
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SIM_GLITCH] Metastable grant detected on CLK_B
expected_root_cause: Grant signals are passed from Arbiter Clock Domain A to Slave Clock Domain B without synchronizers.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk_b) begin q1 <= a_gnt; q2 <= q1; end assign b_gnt_sync = q2;
description: Timing failure when the arbiter and its target operate on different clock trees.

title: Vacuous Fairness Assertion
testbench: task check(); #100; // No requests driven endtask
expected_root_cause: The SVA property `req |-> ##[1:10] gnt` is succeeding vacuously because `req` is never asserted by the testbench.
fix_replacement: assert property (req |-> ##[1:10] gnt); cover property (req);
description: False sense of verification security due to lack of stimulus coverage.

title: Sticky Grant Termination on WR_READY
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [PROT] GNT revoked after address phase but before response
expected_root_cause: The arbiter considers the transaction 'done' once the address is accepted, but AXI-Lite requires the grant to be held until the response channel completes.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (gnt_active && !(bvalid && bready)) gnt <= gnt; else gnt <= 0;
description: Arbiter violates AXI-Lite write protocol by revoking access before the write response.

title: Priority Mask Shadowing Master 0
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [STARVE] Master 0 never served under high load
rtl: assign mask = (1 << last_gnt); // Error: should be (1 << (last_gnt+1)) - 1
expected_root_cause: The mask only blocks the *current* master, but does not correctly prioritize the *next* master in the circle.
fix_replacement: always_comb begin mask_low = (1 << (last_gnt + 1)) - 1; masked_req = req & ~mask_low; end
description: Faulty mask logic creates a 'hole' where Master 0 is unintentionally deprioritized.

title: Illegal 4-State Grant Propagation
expected_root_cause: The grant signal is declared as 'logic' but never assigned a default 0, allowing 'X' to propagate through the design.
fix_replacement: always_comb begin gnt = 0; case(state) ... endcase end
description: X-propagation error caused by uninitialized logic variables.

title: Arbiter Case-Inside Wildcard Collision
expected_root_cause: Using 'inside' with wildcards without 'unique' or 'priority' allows multiple matches to behave non-deterministically based on tool implementation.
fix_replacement: unique case (req) inside 4'b1000: ... 4'b0100: ... endcase
description: Wildcard overlap in case statements causes non-deterministic arbitration.

title: Round-Robin Loop-Back Starvation
expected_root_cause: The loop-back logic only increments if a grant occurs, but if Master 3 is the only one requesting and the arbiter is stuck, the loop-back never triggers.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (gnt_done) last_gnt <= next_ptr;
description: Rotation logic fails to update when only high-index masters are requesting.

title: Grant Stickiness Revoked on RESET_N pulse
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [PROTOCOL] Grant dropped mid-transfer due to reset glitch
rtl: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if (!rst_n) gnt <= 0;
testbench: initial begin #50 force rst_n = 0; #1 release rst_n; end
expected_root_cause: The design uses an unfiltered asynchronous reset. A narrow glitch on the reset line revokes the grant and corrupts the active bus transaction.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) begin r1 <= rst_n; r2 <= r1; end assign safe_rst_n = r2;
description: Glitch on asynchronous reset line causes protocol-violating grant deassertion.

title: Moore Output Mismatch (Latency)
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_VAL] GNT expected cycle 10, Got cycle 11
expected_root_cause: The grant is registered based on the *current* state, creating a 1-cycle lag between the arbiter decision and the physical grant signal.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) gnt <= (next_state == GNT_STATE); // Lookahead
description: One-cycle latency in grant assertion causes performance degradation and monitor errors.

title: Incomplete Priority Rotation (3 Masters)
expected_root_cause: Bitwise inversion used for rotation instead of an incrementer, limiting the arbiter to only two masters despite being instantiated for three.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) last_gnt <= (last_gnt == 2) ? 0 : last_gnt + 1;
description: Rotation logic failure in non-binary master counts.

title: Sequential Priority Encoder Race
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: GNT switched M0->M1 in same cycle as REQ1 rose
expected_root_cause: The priority encoder is implemented inside a sequential block, making it sensitive to input arrival time rather than the clocked request state.
fix_replacement: always_comb begin case(req) ... endcase end
description: Race condition in sequential grant logic violates cycle-accuracy.

title: Arbiter False Positive on Non-Driven ID
expected_root_cause: A hardcoded offset in the grant index address logic points to non-existent master IDs.
fix_replacement: assign gnt = (1 << last_gnt);
description: Out-of-bounds array access in grant vector generation.

title: Grant-Done Handshake Overlap
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [PROT] Multiple GNTs on cycle 50 (Rotation overlapped)
rtl: if (done) gnt <= next_gnt; // next_gnt is already high
expected_root_cause: The new grant is asserted in the same cycle as the 'done' signal of the previous master, causing a 1-cycle bus collision.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (done) gnt <= 0; else if (idle) gnt <= next_gnt;
description: Protocol violation where the new bus owner is granted access before the old owner releases the bus.

title: Priority Inversion via Weights
expected_root_cause: The weighted round-robin registers are incorrectly initialized, giving Master 1 a thousand-to-one advantage over Master 0.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (!rst_n) weights <= '1;
description: Unintended priority bias caused by incorrect weight parameterization.

title: Arbiter X-State on Request Bus
expected_root_cause: Arbiter logic uses '==' which evaluates to false on 'X', potentially granting Master 1 when Master 0's request is indeterminate.
fix_replacement: always_comb begin assert (!$isunknown(req)); ... end
description: Reliability failure where invalid input levels cause incorrect grant decisions.

title: Sequential Grant-Ready Deadlock
expected_root_cause: The arbiter won't grant the bus unless the slave is ready, but the slave won't be ready until it sees a valid grant/address, creating a circular dependency.
fix_replacement: always_comb gnt = calculated_gnt; // Break sequential loop
description: Deadlock caused by making grant assertion dependent on slave readiness.

title: Modport Input-Output Contention
rtl: modport arbiter (output gnt); assign gnt = 1'b0; // External driver also exists
expected_root_cause: Interface modport allows the arbiter to drive a signal that is also driven by the testbench/top-level logic.
fix_replacement: Use unique net names or tri-state logic for multi-driver nets.
description: Physical connection error resulting in signal 'X' due to contention.

title: Sticky Grant Failure on RVALID
expected_root_cause: Grant is revoked after the read address is accepted, but the bus must be held until the read data returns (RVALID).
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (gnt && !rvalid) gnt <= gnt; else gnt <= next;
description: Arbiter breaks read transaction by revoking grant before data is received.

title: Arbiter Priority Jump Bug
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [FAIRNESS] Master 0 granted twice in row with pending reqs
expected_root_cause: Priority register is hardcoded to reset to Master 0 after every grant, violating round-robin rotation.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (done) last_gnt <= winner_id;
description: Deterministic bias towards Master 0 due to logic reset error.

title: Vacuous $rose Assertion
expected_root_cause: Assertion checks `$rose(gnt)`, but since `req` is held high, `gnt` never 'rises' after the first cycle, making the property vacuous for subsequent checks.
fix_replacement: assert property (gnt && active |-> ...);
description: Poorly written assertion fails to check stability, only transitions.

title: Arbiter Clock-Enable Missing
rtl: always_ff @(posedge clk) state <= next_state; // No enable
expected_root_cause: Sequential block lacks a clock-enable gate, causing the arbiter to transition even when the rest of the system is stalled.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (clk_en) state <= next_state;
description: Synchronization failure where the arbiter drifts from the system clock enable.

title: Mask Bit-Inversion Starvation
rtl: assign masked_req = req & mask; // mask is 2'b01
expected_root_cause: Mask logic is bitwise ANDed when it should be ANDed with the *inverted* mask to block the previous winner.
fix_replacement: always_comb masked_req = req & ~(1 << last_gnt);
description: Logic error in request masking prevents fairness rotation.

title: Arbiter False Grant on Reset N Glitch
expected_root_cause: Grant signal is tied to the inverse of reset, causing a full-cycle illegal bus grant while the system is resetting.
fix_replacement: always_comb gnt = rst_n ? calculated : 0;
description: Active-high grant logic incorrectly triggered by active-low reset signal.

title: Priority Mask Offset Error
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: Master 0 granted twice with M1-M3 pending
expected_root_cause: The mask `(1 << 0) - 1` is 0, so Master 0 is not blocked even though it was the last winner.
fix_replacement: always_comb mask = (1 << (last_gnt + 1)) - 1;
description: Off-by-one error in mask generation allows immediate re-granting of the same master.

title: Non-One-Hot Grant during State Transition
rtl: always_comb begin gnt = (state == S0); gnt[5] = (next_state == S1); end
expected_root_cause: One grant bit is driven by the *current* state and another by the *next* state, causing both to be high during the transition cycle.
fix_replacement: always_comb begin gnt = 0; if (state == S0) gnt=1; else if (state == S1) gnt[5]=1; end
description: Structural error in grant decoding leads to bus collisions during FSM hops.

title: Arbiter Internal Counter Overflow
rtl: logic [7:0] count; // Needs 9 bits for 256 window
expected_root_cause: The starvation watchdog counter wraps back to zero before hitting the fairness threshold, effectively disabling the alert.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (pending) count <= count + 1; else count <= 0;
description: Watchdog failure due to bit-width limitation in the fairness monitor.

title: Grant Stickiness Latch Inference
rtl: always_comb if (active) sticky_gnt = gnt; // No else
expected_root_cause: The stickiness logic fails to define the signal value when 'active' is low, creating a latch that holds old grants indefinitely.
fix_replacement: always_comb begin sticky_gnt = 0; if (active) sticky_gnt = gnt; end
description: Inadvertent hardware register creation leads to persistent bus ownership.

title: Arbiter Priority Sync Failure (Async Req)
rtl: assign gnt = req & mask; // req is asynchronous
expected_root_cause: Asynchronous request signals are sampled directly into combinational logic, causing glitches on the grant output when the request arrives near the clock edge.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) begin r_q <= req; gnt <= r_q & mask; end
description: Lack of input synchronization leads to unstable grant outputs.

title: Round-Robin Reverse Priority Error
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: Grant sequence is M3, M2, M1, M0
expected_root_cause: Arbiter decrements the pointer instead of incrementing, which may be functionally 'fair' but violates the forward-rotation specification.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (done) last_gnt <= (last_gnt + 1) % 4;
description: Counter-clockwise rotation violates protocol-ordered sequencing.

title: Arbiter Grant-Done Glitch (delta cycle)
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [ARB] Contention detected (M0 done, M1 start)
expected_root_cause: Combinational logic uses the 'done' signal to immediately grant the next master, but the previous master hasn't physically released the bus wires until the next clock edge.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (done) gnt <= next; else if (busy) gnt <= gnt;
description: Zero-delay simulation race condition resulting in 1-cycle bus contention.

title: Arbiter Illegal ID Return
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_DATA] Master ID mismatch in transaction
testbench: task check(); drive_m0(); // SBD expects ID 0, got 1 endtask
expected_root_cause: The arbiter reports the Master ID with an incorrect offset to the scoreboard, causing false-positive verification errors.
fix_replacement: assign trans.id = winner_index;
description: ID tag corruption in the monitor-to-scoreboard path.

title: Arbiter Power-On State Bias
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [FAIRNESS] Master 0 always wins first transaction
expected_root_cause: The fairness register starts at 0 by default, ensuring Master 0 always has an advantage in the very first transaction after power-up.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if (!rst_n) last_gnt <= 2'b00;
description: Lack of power-on randomization/initialization creates a predictable startup bias.
